title: Ensure soft delete on Recovery Services vaults is Enabled

input_row.title: Ensure soft delete on Recovery Services vaults is Enabled
input_row.description: Soft delete provides additional protection for Recovery Services vault data. With soft delete enabled, deleted backup data can be recovered within the retention period.
input_row.rationale: Important backup data could be accidentally deleted or removed by a malicious actor. With soft delete enabled, data is retained for at least 14 days before permanent deletion, allowing for the recovery of the backup data.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a Recovery Services vault. 3. Under Settings, click Properties. 4. Under Soft Delete and security settings, click Update. 5. Ensure the box next to Enable soft delete for cloud workloads is checked. 6. Ensure the box next to Enable soft delete and security settings for hybrid workloads is checked. 7. Ensure that the Soft delete retention period is set to an appropriate value between 14 and 180, inclusive. 8. Repeat steps 1-7 for each Recovery Services vault. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: az backup vault list For each Recovery Services vault, run the following command: az backup vault show --resource-group --name Ensure that under softDeleteSettings, softDeleteState is set to Enabled, enhancedSecurityState is set to Enabled, and softDeleteRetentionPeriodInDays is set to an appropriate value between 14 and 180, inclusive. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 31b8092a-36b8-434b-9af7-5ec844364148 • Name: '[Preview]: Soft delete must be enabled for Recovery Services Vaults.'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Recovery Services vaults. 2. Click the name of a Recovery Services vault. 3. Under Settings, click Properties. 4. Under Soft Delete and security settings, click Update. 5. Check the box next to Enable soft delete for cloud workloads. 6. Check the box next to Enable soft delete and security settings for hybrid workloads. 7. In the box next to Soft delete retention period, set an appropriate number of days for deleted data to be retained, between 14 and 180, inclusive. 8. If it is appropriate for your organization, check the box next to Enable always- on soft delete. Note: Once enabled, this setting cannot be disabled. 9. If enabling always-on soft delete, check the box next to Confirm enabling always-on soft delete. 10. Click Update. 11. Repeat steps 1-10 for each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list Recovery Services vaults: az backup vault list For each Recovery Services vault requiring remediation, run the following command to enable soft delete and set an appropriate number of days for deleted data to be retained, between 14 and 180, inclusive: az backup vault backup-properties set --resource-group -- name --soft-delete-feature-state Enable --hybrid- backup-security-features Enable --soft-delete-duration Note: To enable always-on soft delete, the above command can be executed with -- soft-delete-feature-state AlwaysOn. Once enabled, this setting cannot be disabled.
input_row.impact: There is no additional cost for backup data in the soft delete state for up to and including 14 days. However, retention beyond 14 days may incur additional charges.
input_row.default_value: Soft delete is enabled by default on newly created Recovery Services vaults.
input_row.additional_information: Azure Backup soft delete protection is available for the following services: • Azure virtual machines • SQL server in Azure VM and SAP HANA in Azure VM workloads

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_backup_recovery_services_vault_soft_delete_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use**: 
  - You can use the `boto3` library to interact with the Backup service in AWS.
  - Specifically, you might need to retrieve details about backup vault settings using:
    - `boto3.client('backup').list_backup_vaults()`
    - `boto3.client('backup').describe_backup_vault(BackupVaultName='your_vault_name')`

- **AWS CLI commands (if applicable)**:
  - Use the AWS CLI to describe backup vaults:
    ```
    aws backup list-backup-vaults
    aws backup describe-backup-vault --backup-vault-name your_vault_name
    ```

- **Specific resources to enumerate**: 
  - This should enumerate all backup vaults in the specified AWS account and region.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate**: 
  - Check if the property `softDeleteEnabled` is set to `true` and if the `softDeleteRetentionDays` is within the range of 14 to 180 days.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values**: 
  - **Pass**: 
    - `softDeleteEnabled` is `true`
    - `softDeleteRetentionDays` is between 14 and 180 (inclusive)
  - **Fail**: 
    - `softDeleteEnabled` is `false` or 
    - `softDeleteRetentionDays` is less than 14 or greater than 180

- **Example**: 
  - "Use boto3 `describe_backup_vault` to verify `softDeleteSettings`. Check that `softDeleteState` is enabled and that the retention period is appropriate."
